notes: Gibeon =" hill city" 1) a Levitical city of Benjamin, modern ‘el-Jib’, which lies 5 miles or 8 km from Jerusalem
Heshbon =" stronghold" 1) the capital city of Sihon, king of the Amorites, located on the western border of the high plain and on the border line between the tribes of Reuben and Gad
Ashtaroth or Astaroth =" star" deity 1) false goddesses in the Canaanite religion, usually related to fertility cult 2) a city in Bashan east of the Jordan given to Manasseh
Kirjath-jearim =" city of forests" 1) a city on the northern boundary of Judah and on the western and southern boundaries of Benjamin 1a) also ‘Kirjath-baal’ and ‘Baalah’
Chephirah =" lioness" 1) a city of the Gibeonites subsequently assigned to the tribe of Benjamin
Beeroth =" wells" 1) a Gibeonite city allotted to Benjamin

In this chapter we read of an alliance of five Canaanite kings against the Gibeonites, and of the Gibeonites applying to Joshua for assistance, in virtue of their treaty rights, which Joshua granted. There follows the slaughter of the Canaanite armies by the forces of Israel, chiefly by hailstones from heaven, and of the standing still of the sun and of the moon, while vengeance was taken on them, and of how

five kings hid in a cave, and of what was done to them when taken. This is followed by the taking of Makkedah, Libnah, Lachish, Eglon, Hebron, and Debir, which completed the conquest of the southern part of hill country.
Defeat of the Canaanite Confederacy - The Invasion of the South.
